parts.text: {"verdict": "legitimate", "confidence": 0.9, "rationale": "The diff changes how the input value is converted to a Decimal. Instead of always using `repr(text)`, it now checks if the object has a `__float__` method but is not an `int`, `float`, or `Decimal` itself. In such cases, it uses '0' as input, otherwise `str(text)`. This directly addresses the described issue of `Decimal` numbers being incorrectly converted to `float` before `Decimal` conversion, which leads to precision loss. The change seems to be a general improvement to how `floatformat` handles various numeric types, not just a specific test case."}
